title: iberis amara subsp. peyerimhoffii
intro: Iberis amara subsp. peyerimhoffii, also known as the Algerian candytuft, is a subspecies of the Iberis amara plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and soothing digestive problems. Iberis amara subsp. peyerimhoffii can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine in some parts of the world, often in combination with other herbs.
